title: hypericum elongatum subsp. tymphresteum
intro: Hypericum elongatum subsp. tymphresteum, also known as Tymphresteum St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing stress and inflammation, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote relaxation.
